title: Project Member Lookup Error Schema
description: Error envelope for the project-membership picker lookup/search endpoint. Pins the small set of error codes the FE handles inline (before issuing POST /projects/{project_id}/memberships). The envelope intentionally never echoes user_id, email, q, or any other lookup input back to the caller, so a denied or not-found response cannot be used to enumerate users in another tenant.

error_code.description: Stable code the FE switches on. PROJECT_CONTEXT_REQUIRED / PROJECT_NOT_FOUND / PROJECT_ACCESS_DENIED mirror project_context_error_schema for cross-endpoint consistency. INSUFFICIENT_PERMISSIONS is returned when the caller is authenticated for the project but lacks the project:membership:manage permission. INVALID_LOOKUP_QUERY is returned when neither q nor email is provided, when both are provided, or when q/email fail format/length checks. USER_NOT_FOUND is returned only by the single-user variant (email= exact lookup with require_match=true); the list variant returns 200 with items=[] instead.

message.description: Human-readable message safe for inline rendering. MUST NOT include the requested email, user_id, or query string; that would let a caller probe other tenants by reading back the echoed value.

permission_required.description: Set to the missing permission name (e.g. 'project:membership:manage') when error_code is INSUFFICIENT_PERMISSIONS; null otherwise.

project_id.description: Echo of the project path parameter on PROJECT_* errors so the FE can correlate the error with the picker context. Null on non-project-context errors.
